issues.title: Text selection vertical extent should be defined per line (not per glyph)
issues.body:
As [reported by @_mono on Twitter]( https://twitter.com/_mono/status/1023733699614060545), the Flutter text selection highlight rect should be computed at the line level as opposed to per glyph. To see this effect, paste the sequence `(　´･‿･｀)` into a Flutter text field and select it.

Example images below:

Flutter selection:
![flutter](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/45705368-424c8180-bb2e-11e8-9502-3fa404afc066.png)

iOS OEM selection:
![ios](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/45705373-45e00880-bb2e-11e8-930a-156f02e88a15.png)

Android OEM selection:
![android](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/45706301-bdaf3280-bb30-11e8-9ee6-07384c82395c.png)
comments: /cc @jason-simmons @garymm @xster

Based on the platform-specific behaviour, it looks like a bug to me. Both Android and iOS appear to use the line height. 

It seems potentially useful to expose the glyph-specific metrics (e.g. if you were interested in computing text metrics of a glyph independent from its context) but in terms of selection, I'd assume we should be using line-height.
Looks like the linked commit(s) were intended to support text _spans_ being treated as a single block for Rich text, but is now treating each glyph as a separate block.
Did you mean to CC someone else? I have no particular expertise or interest in this bug.
Should've been @GaryQian

I think I originally implemented it so that the height is the max of each line to match native/blink behavior. I believe this is mainly so that multi-line highlights do not leave awkward gaps between lines due to differing heights (as seen below).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1887398/45896986-b08d8000-bd8a-11e8-9d90-6f63afaa8994.png)

When spans of differing heights are stacked on top of each other, they always advance enough in the y direction to accommodate the tallest span

Also, @dnfield it seems that the (　´･‿･｀) text uses characters from different sets, so they are automatically broken into different spans. it just happens that they are alternating, making it look particularly strange.

So it sounds like we're treating it as different spans because of different sets.

I think what a user would expect (and what Android/iOS do here), is that the text runs are _styled_ the same, so they should be treated as the same height.  In other words, this text run was built from a single `ParagraphBuilder.addText` with no intervening `pushStyle` or `pop` - or, could be generated using a single `TextSpan` class.

That makes sense to me, but to match native behavior (and therefore expected), I think we still should take the max of the line heights. We could also provide the glyph/span specific heights as a separate API, but not sure if that would be particularly useful.

On native text, even if you have a font size 10 on the same line as a font size 150, the bounding boxes for that line are 150 high.
https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/6335 begins to implement a new API for more advanced GetRectsForRange, which provides the boxes for text selection.
https://github.com/flutter/engine/commit/3a9c22afde676bee3c455749101757c1814153fd has landed, but still needs to be enabled on the framework side.

https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/6644 adds API for various types of enclosing boxes. `Paragraph.getBoxesForRange(...)` takes a BoxHeightStyle and a BoxWidthStyle which define how the boxes are calculated.

The default has been left as `tight` so the current behavior will continue, but for apps that desire other behavior, custom styles can be presented to the `getBoxesForRange` method.

This also allows emulating different text selection behaviors present on mac, windows, ios, android, and so on.

@cbracken 

As commented at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/23433#issuecomment-616003053, this isn’t fixed yet.
I can’t figure out why this was closed.
Could you re-open this to track the issue appropriately?

This thread has been automatically locked since there has not been any recent activity after it was closed. If you are still experiencing a similar issue, please open a new bug, including the output of `flutter doctor -v` and a minimal reproduction of the issue.
This doesn't appear to be fixed. /cc @GaryQian I'm guessing that we're mixing a framework PR to take advantage of the new API that was added in #6644.

Issue replicable on latest stable (1.20.2)

![21997](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67046386/91826041-b2d90400-ec5a-11ea-82b4-b1688fe3606a.png)

issues.title: [Windows] TextField Cannot Completely Disable System IME
issues.body:
### Use case

Flutter's TextField widget lacks proper control over system Input Method Editors (IME). While developers can configure keyboard layouts and input formatting, there's no reliable way to prevent IME activation - especially problematic for CJK languages where IMEs automatically engage.



### Proposal

Current properties like keyboardType, textInputAction, and inputFormatters only control keyboard appearance and text filtering, but cannot prevent IME composition states. This breaks applications requiring direct keyboard input only (OTP/PIN entry, terminals, command interfaces).

Numeric-only fields still trigger IME composition
Real-time validation disrupted by IME intermediate states
Inconsistent behavior across platforms
No workaround for CJK language environments
comments: I have created a plugin called "force_english_ime": ^0.0.3. You can give it a try. Hope this helps you.

@li986726488-dotcom would you be able to post a minimal repro of a numeric field triggering IME composition, and specify the platform(s) that you see it on? If that's different than native then that's a bug and not just a proposal.

I wonder how this is handled on native. I'm not sure how to prevent the IME from handling composition.
The `triaged-framework` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-framework` label or `fyi-framework` label.

> I have created a plugin called "force_english_ime": ^0.0.3. You can give it a try. Hope this helps you.

@W2XiaoYu Thank you for your help. I have used a similar approach to disable IME using the system API.

(or visit https://flutter.dev/to/windows-android-setup for detailed instructions). If the Android SDK has been installed to a custom location, please use `flutter config --android-sdk` to update to that location. [√] Chrome - develop for the web [√] Visual Studio - develop Windows apps (Visual Studio Community 2022 17.14.13 (August 2025)) [!] Android Studio (not installed) [√] Connected device (3 available) [√] Network resources @justinmc In my use case, I only need to input English letters and numbers. However, users can switch to Chinese or other language input methods, which pops up an unwanted IME candidate window. This doesn't meet my expectations. It would be great if there was a property to directly disable IME. Many apps in the market typically don't allow switching to other input methods when entering passwords.

issues.title: Supporting platform accessibility actions for custom keyboards
issues.body:
## Use case
I'm trying to implement a custom keyboard and want to match accessibility actions of framework keyboards of Android/iOS but am unable to do so.

I'm using following `Semantics` for keys of custom keyboard.
```dart
Semantics.fromProperties(
  properties: SemanticsProperties(
    label: "some-semantics-label",
    keyboardKey: true,
)
```

### Expected Behaviour

**Android**
When Talkback is on, Android system keyboard doesn't use double tap to input the key instead when you tap on the key, its semantic label is pronounced by Talkback and the key is entered into the input field. On Flutter using `keyboardKey: true` on our custom widget does not reproduce similar behaviour and I'm unable to find a way to remove double tap behaviour on Android. 

Here's a video which demonstrates android's system keyboard accessbiility: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y2tT4jDRfWs

On Native Android, we achieve this using following piece of code
```kotlin
ViewCompat.setAccessibilityDelegate(view, object : AccessibilityDelegateCompat() {
    override fun onInitializeAccessibilityNodeInfo(host: View, info: AccessibilityNodeInfoCompat) {
        super.onInitializeAccessibilityNodeInfo(host, info)
        info.removeAction(AccessibilityNodeInfoCompat.AccessibilityActionCompat.ACTION_CLICK)
        info.isClickable = false
    }
})
```

**iOS**
On iOS, the default behaviour of system keyboard, when voice over is on, is to pronounce the key semantic label but activate it only on double tap. However this behaviour can be overriden in voice over settings by turning on a setting called `touch typing`, after which the key can be input using a single tap. Again using `keyboardKey: true` on our custom widget does not reproduce similar behaviour instead it always requires double tap to input key irrespective of voice over touch typing setting.

Here's a video which demonstrates various modes of iOS's system keyboard with accessibility: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1O2hwY-mGyQ

On Native iOS, we achieve this by using `keyboardKey` accessibility trait
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiaccessibility/uiaccessibilitytraits/1620182-keyboardkey

**Note**
Full sample code: https://github.com/jitinsharma/flutter_custom_keyboard/blob/master/lib/main.dart#L115

## Proposal
The expectation is `keyboardKey: true` should follow accessibility actions of framework keyboards on each Platform, which would help in replicating these actions on any flutter widget.
comments: @chunhtai FYI This might just be a matter of plumbing the trait up to the framework.

This trait was originally added for fuchsia use case and are not used by any other platform
It looks like in iOS we should set the accessibilityTrait to https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiaccessibilitytraitkeyboardkey?language=objc

in Android
we need to set
https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/accessibility/AccessibilityNodeInfo#setTextEntryKey(boolean)

issues.title: Image Keyboard for other platforms
issues.body: ### Is there an existing issue for this? - [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues) - [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports) ### Use case https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/20796 has been marked as fixed by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/110052 and is closed & locked now, but https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/110052 implemented it only for Android; on iOS, Web, and other platforms, `contentInsertionConfiguration` hasn't been implemented, and I think this should be tracked somewhere. - Web: [The demo on the api reference page](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/EditableText/contentInsertionConfiguration.html) doesn't work, and the page doesn't mention the fact that it's implemented only for Android yet - iOS: I tried copy&pasting an image into a textfield with `contentInsertionConfiguration` configured, but it just shows the raw data without invoking `onContentInserted` - ### Proposal - Update the api reference page to indicate that `contentInsertionConfiguration` is only implemented for Android yet - Track the implementation of image keyboard feature for iOS and other platforms - Related: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/56615542/how-to-allow-and-get-memoji-stickers-in-a-text-field - It talks about handling Memoji sticker (= png image) input on iOS ### Related issues - https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/23603 - https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/32045
comments:
> * Web: [The demo on the api reference page](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/EditableText/contentInsertionConfiguration.html) doesn't work, and the page doesn't mention the fact that it's implemented only for Android yet



> * Update the api reference page to indicate that `contentInsertionConfiguration` is only implemented for Android yet

@Shawn-sudo 

Per `ContentInsertionConfiguration` class [documentation](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/ContentInsertionConfiguration-class.html), it does mention that following properties are used for Android only:

`onContentInserted` : https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/ContentInsertionConfiguration/onContentInserted.html
`allowedMimeTypes`: https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/ContentInsertionConfiguration/allowedMimeTypes.html

As for support for other platforms (web, ios), treating this as a proposal as applicable.
Thanks for opening this!  You're right that we don't seem to have anywhere that we were tracking image keyboard support for other platforms, good catch.  It is something we want to support.
Any News regarding this issue?

issues.title: Why is DropdownButtonFormField's onChanged required?
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

Try to initialize a `DropdownButtonFormField` without specifying the `onChanged` parameter.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

For `onChanged` to not be required and the widget to still work, which is consistent with other FormField widgets, such as `TextFormField`.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

Currently the `onChanged` parameter is required and is seemingly used as a way to disable the widget if you set it to null. If you don't care about the onChanged event and just want to use the DropdownButtonFormField in a normal basic form, you still have to set onChanged to an empty function (e.g. `onChanged: (value) {}`) otherwise it won't update the FormState/stay enabled. This feels unintuitive to me since e.g. the `TextFormField` widget does not require you to specify `onChanged` unless you're interested in that event.

While I'm not that experienced with Flutter, it seems like it would make more sense to not use `onChanged` as a way of disabling a `DropdownButtonFormField` and instead let it purely be a way of letting the user do something when the field changes. I don't know if there are issues with that or if this odd current behavior is just a leftover from old logic? Seems easy enough to change it up (I could attempt to submit a PR for it).

**Proposed Changes:**
Add a `bool enabled` parameter to `DropdownButtonFormField` (consistent with TextFormField) and use that to decide whether to set the underlying `DropdownButton.onChanged` to control its enabled'ness.

In the future, it might make sense to add an `enabled` parameter to `DropdownButton` as well, as an explicit way of controlling whether it should be enabled or not.
comments: This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

@justinmc I’m looking into this proposal, and technically it’s straightforward. The main challenge would be refactoring some tests, which in this case means adding the `enabled` parameter in a few places and deciding whether it should be `true` or `false`.
If that’s not an issue, I can start working on it.

@justinmc @Piinks I’ve prepared an implementation and submitted it as a draft. However, I’m unsure whether the impact justifies the change.

Patterns such as `onChanged: condition ? (value) { ... } : null` would be affected, resulting in a small breaking change. What would be the best way to proceed?

issues.title: Easier use of in-app keyboards with TextInputControl
comments: Is this a duplicate of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/29823?

Tell me if this is right: You'd like to build something that looks like a system keyboard using Flutter, use it in your Flutter app only, and have it easily plug into TextField et. al.

Have you seen TextInputControl, introduced in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/76072? Not sure how much of this issue is covered by that but I want to narrow down this request in relation to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/29823 and existing work.

Hey @justinmc .

> You'd like to build something that looks like a system keyboard

Anything, can be similar, can be completely custom made.

> use it in your Flutter app only

yes

> and have it easily plug into TextField

yes, **easily**

I have looked at TextInputControl and [this demo](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextInputControl-mixin.html). 

In this particular example it looks about right, but imagine you have two text fields - one needs a regular QWERTY keyboard, the other the custom keyboard, switching between two as user interacts with the form - requires tedious code and difficult to achieve smooth screen resizing.
Got it, so let's keep this issue open as it relates to a better experience using an in-app keyboard with multiple fields. I've updated the title to match.

issues.title: TextFormField (and TextField) widgets do not wrap text correctly
issues.body: Similar to the issues reported in #18761, the input fields in Flutter suffer from the same disfunction. Currently, the text input fields in Flutter do not wrap their input correctly. Instead of breaking a word in the middle and wrapping the remainder to the next line, Flutter is breaking the string at the word boundary closest to the end of the line, which is non buono. #### Flutter #### Chrome Web browsers correctly split the string at the character that would otherwise overflow. #### Code snippet ```dart TextFormField( decoration: InputDecoration(border: OutlineInputBorder()), minLines: null, maxLines: null, initialValue: 'https://www.example.com/some/thingweirdisgoingon.here', ) ```

issues.title: [Desktop] Multimedia key events not reported to HardwareKeyboard
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Add the following to a root level StatelessWidget:

```dart
@override
void initState() {
  super.initState();
  HardwareKeyboard.instance.addHandler(
    (e) {
      print(e);
      return false;
    },
  );
}
```

2. Build to a desktop target (I tested versus macOS).
3. Press most keys, see print statements.
4. Press F-keys, see print statements.
5. Press Multimedia keys (On macOS, same physical keys as F-keys, but with FN-toggle), see nothing printed.

As these are system events, it makes sense that most would not be sent to the app (screen brightness, volume, etc). But it's common for media player apps to require access to Play/Pause, Forward, and Backward keypresses.


**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

I expected to be able to receive keyboard events for media playback keypresses. These must be registered with each OS - e.g., currently on my machine, Spotify is receiving those events and toggling playback regardless of what window has focus. Flutter should be able to similarly register for these privileged events.


<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

```
Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
```

issues.title: Material Component widget request: multi-select search field with auto-suggestion
issues.body:
## Use case
[RecipeRadar](https://www.reciperadar.com/) currently provides the user with the ability to enter multiple ingredients and/or kitchen equipment items that they would like to include or exclude when searching for recipes.

This functionality is built using a server-side auto-suggestion API that responds to the user's query text with suggested valid ingredient and equipment identifiers (and their corresponding human-readable names).

While the user-facing application does not use `flutter`, we are evaluating it as a possible next-generation application framework, particularly as [support for Progressive Web Apps](https://flutter.dev/web) as a target platform continues to develop.

One detractor that we have encountered is that `flutter` does not yet support a multi-selection auto-suggest search input.

We are currently using [select2's multi-select functionality](https://select2.org/getting-started/basic-usage#multi-select-boxes-pillbox) and have discovered existing native client-side functionality in [Android's MultiAutoCompleteTextView](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/widget/MultiAutoCompleteTextView).

## Proposal
A [Material Component widget](https://flutter.dev/docs/development/ui/widgets/material) that [implements](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/0549ab239009e6f76f95881364e935cd345a4da7/packages/flutter/test/material) support for multi-valued text input, with support for auto-suggestion.

- [Android's MultiAutoCompleteTextView](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/widget/MultiAutoCompleteTextView) may provide a useful reference for design, accessibility and implementation concerns
- Support for population of values from a server-side API endpoint is required; we and likely other customers would be greatly aided during system migration if the component would support client-side transformation of the data returned by the endpoint
- User-selectde items will require both a user-accessible text description, and also a service-defined ID value (similar to the distinction between an HTML [option](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/option) field's innerHTML and its `value` attribute)
- Support for purely client-side autosuggestion and tokenization may be valuable for other customers (although it is not currently a requirement that we have)

## Relates to
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/17119
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/23827
comments: It would be useful if this worked with chips copying the functionality of the gmail To/BCC/CC fields as you type with a separator character or a selection that goes and puts in the separator. All of the pub.dev packages that implement something like this are of very low quality.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

issues.title: Please design as public interface/Mixing instead of passing RenderEditable everywhere
comments: @LongCatIsLooong  @justinmc @YeungKC

@zmtzawqlp 
Please check [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/80226#issuecomment-818292098) comment for your reference and see if it resonates with your request.
